student_verification.name: student
student_verification.declaration: I used Claude to help implement the failure mode demonstration. I chose sarcasm as the failure scenario myself because it directly connects to Assumption 1 from my Task 1.2 — emoticons as reliable sentiment signals. The suggested modification is my own reasoning about what would fix the root cause.

interactions.response_summary: Clarified there is no Task 3.3 (Question 3 has only 3.1 and 3.2), then proposed sarcasm/irony as the failure scenario since it directly violates Assumption 1 from Task 1.2.

interactions.prompt: ohk so like how do we actually show the model failing — do we need a real dataset or can we construct one
interactions.response_summary: Explained that constructing a small synthetic sarcasm dataset is cleaner and more controlled — we know exactly which tweets should fail and why, which makes the analysis more interpretable than a noisy scraped dataset.

interactions.prompt: ok makes sense...but is 25 tweets enough to demonstrate the failure convincingly
interactions.response_summary: Explained that 25 is enough for a demonstration because the failure is deterministic — every tweet with a conflicting emoticon will be systematically misclassified in the same direction. The confusion matrix will show this clearly even with a small sample.

interactions.prompt: ok can you now write the full notebook for this
interactions.response_summary: Built the full task_3_2 notebook — pipeline rebuild, model training on normal tweets, sarcastic dataset construction, failure demonstration with per-tweet breakdown, bar chart and confusion matrix saved to partB/results/.
interactions.student_modification: Reviewed the sarcastic tweet examples and added a few of my own to make the dataset feel more natural.

interactions.prompt: the suggested modification — can it be more specific like actually rooted in the paper not just generic
interactions.response_summary: Revised the suggested fix to reference the paper's emoticon-to-tag substitution step specifically — down-weight ||P|| when surrounding polar words conflict, rather than generic 'context-aware sentiment' phrasing.

interactions.prompt: also in the failure explanation can we mention that the error is directional not random
interactions.response_summary: Added a sentence to the interpretation noting that the confusion matrix shows directional errors (negative predicted as positive in the emoticon direction) confirming the failure is systematic, not noise.

top_5_prompts.why_selected: Led to the decision to use a synthetic controlled sarcasm dataset
